channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: After layoffs and funding problems, Head Start leaders fear what comes next
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration on Tuesday laid off scores of Health and Human Services employees, including many who ran the Head Start early education program.
item.dc:creator: Moriah Balingit, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dems mum on Trump's court fights despite trying to limit Biden-blocking judges
item.description: Democrats are quiet now that nationwide injunctions are limiting Trump, but during Biden’s presidency, they introduced legislation to address it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden-appointed judge blocks Trump admin from terminating legal aid for unaccompanied migrant children
item.description: A federal judge in California has blocked the Trump administration from cutting off legal counsel funding for unaccompanied migrant minors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: District judges' orders blocking Trump agenda face hearing in top Senate committee
item.description: The Senate Judiciary committee will examine federal district judges issuing nationwide orders that are being increasingly frequently levied against the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Sen. Napoleon Harris wins Thornton Township supervisor seat, Jason House victorious in Dolton mayoral race
item.description: Village of Dolton Senior Trustee Jason House won the election for Dolton mayor by a landslide, while State Sen. Napoleon B. Harris III claimed victory to become the Thornton Township Supervisor-elect, unseating the incumbent, self-proclaimed “Super Mayor” Tiffany Henyard.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, WGN 9

item.title: Former DeSantis official defeats Dem for Matt Gaetz's House seat
item.description: A special election in Florida's 1st Congressional District between Republican Jimmy Patronis and Democrat Gay Valimont was held to determine the successor to Matt Gaetz's House seat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican state Sen. Randy Fine wins special election for Florida's 6th Congressional District
item.description: Republican state Sen. Randy Fine won a special election Tuesday to represent Florida's 6th Congressional District, defeating Democratic challenger Josh Weil and squashing efforts by national Democrats who spent millions of dollars on the race.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP state senator wins Florida special election, extends GOP House majority
item.description: Randy Fine won Tuesday night's special election in Florida, and will now hold former Rep. Michael Waltz's seat in the Sunshine State's 6th Congressional District.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top scientists warn that Trump policies are causing a ‘climate of fear’ in research
item.description: Some 2,000 scientists, including dozens of Nobel Prize winners, have signed an open letter warning that the U.S. lead in science is being “decimated” by the Trump administration’s cuts to research.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: FBI to increase personnel in field offices to address violent crimes in Native American communities
item.description: The FBI is sending additional personnel to various field offices to help solve violent crime cases on Native lands, the Justice Department said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Institute of Peace headquarters transfer is a done deal even as lawsuit continues to unfold
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A hearing Tuesday on the fate of the Institute of Peace ended without a legal decision, but presented a deep look into how the Trump administration is remaking the federal government. Multiple times in a Tuesday hearing, U.S.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Melania Trump says courage is 'based in love' as she honors women from around the world for bravery
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Courage is based in love, Melania Trump said Tuesday as she recognized eight women from around the world for bravery, including an Israeli citizen who was held captive by Hamas for nearly two months after the militant group's deadly raid on her country in October 2023.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration cutting some funds to Planned Parenthood
item.description: Nine Planned Parenthood affiliates received notices about the Trump administration’s plans to withhold Title X funding starting April 1, according to the release.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Layoffs begin at U.S. health agencies responsible for research, tracking disease and regulating food
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department began receiving notices of dismissal Tuesday in an overhaul ultimately expected to lay off up to 10,000 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Ohio school district reverts to birth names, catching students, teachers off guard
item.description: Students at Columbus City Schools with preferred names in the district's system had their names reverted back to match their birth certificates.
item.dc:creator: Delaney Ruth, Fox 5

item.title: A Senate vote to reverse Trump's tariffs on Canada is testing Republican support
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With President Donald Trump's so-called “Liberation Day” of tariff implementation fast approaching, Senate Democrats are putting Republican support for some of those plans to the test by forcing a vote to nullify the emergency declaration that underpins the tariffs on Canada.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota union employees demand Walz halt half-time in office policy
item.description: Around 500 Minnesota state employees protested Saturday, calling on DFL Gov. Tim Walz to reverse course on a policy to require more in-office work. The governor says having more employees spend at least half their time at their workplaces will improve communication and collaboration.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Luna accuses House Freedom Caucus of smear; exits group over parental proxy voting battle
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) announced Monday that she is leaving the House Freedom Caucus amid her battle with GOP leadership and hard-line Republicans over her push to allow proxy voting for new parents.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 60 percent disapprove of Trump tariffs: Survey
item.description: Just 38 percent of respondents said they approve of Trump's approach to trade negotiations with other countries.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Adams' lawyer urges ‘prompt resolution’ ahead of election deadline
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – With a filing deadline approaching in New York City’s upcoming mayoral election, Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyer has urged a judge to "promptly" issue a decision in the mayor’s bribery case. Petitions are due this week for candidates to appear on a party's primary ballot.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Wisconsin's attorney general asks the state Supreme Court to stop Musk's $1 million payments
item.description: Wisconsin’s attorney general has asked the liberal-controlled state Supreme Court to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters. The request comes hours before President Donald Trump’s ally plans the giveaway at an evening rally in Green Bay.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
